summary: Iteration 23 — Backfill saved-card endpoints (race-condition recovery). 11/11 pytest PASS at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration23_backfill_saved_card.py covering: (a) auth gates — POST /api/admin/bookings/{id}/backfill-saved-card and POST /api/admin/payments/backfill-saved-cards both return 401/403 without Bearer; (b) single backfill error paths — 404 unknown booking, 400 'not paid' for existing unpaid test booking 5d6c9c49…, 400 'no session id' for seeded paid booking with no payment_session_id and no payment_transactions row, 400 'no payment_method' for seeded paid booking with bogus session_id (Stripe REST returns 404 → _capture_off_session_ids returns {} → handler raises 400 with payment_method message — verified end-to-end); (c) bulk backfill — POST returns 200 with proper shape {backfilled_count:int, skipped_count:int, affected:[], skipped:[{id,cn,reason}]}; both seeded bookings appear in skipped[] with correct reason strings ('session'/'payment_method'); (d) REGRESSION smoke — /api/quote with additional_stops returns quotes[]+distance_miles, /api/admin/bookings list 200, /api/admin/me returns admin email, /api/admin/bookings/{id}/charge-wait-time still 400-gated on no-saved-card booking. CODE REVIEW: verified webhook handler at server.py L2865 calls _capture_off_session_ids(sid) AND merges via update_set.update(ids) so customer+pm IDs land in the same $set when webhook marks booking paid (the fix). Polling endpoint at L2645-2660 also calls _capture_off_session_ids and has explicit 'webhook beat us' branch: if booking already paid AND missing stripe_payment_method_id AND Stripe now returns one → updates the booking with the IDs. _capture_off_session_ids at L2733 uses ?expand[]=payment_intent and returns empty dict on any HTTP failure or exception (no exceptions leak). Seed cleanup verified: 0 TEST_ bookings remain post-run.
action_items: (Carry-over) server.py is now 3959 lines — modularize into routers/{admin,driver,quote,booking,payments,webhook}.py. Backfill endpoints + helper are a natural payments-router slice.

(Optional polish) _capture_off_session_ids returns {} on any failure — admin gets a generic 400 'payment_method not returned'. Consider differentiating Stripe 404 (session expired/unknown) vs auth failure vs payment_intent without payment_method, so admin sees actionable copy. Non-blocking — the current generic message already explains 'method may not be reusable off-session'.

(Nice-to-have) Bulk endpoint /api/admin/payments/backfill-saved-cards has no rate-limit or pagination. For a future production DB with thousands of paid bookings this could thrash Stripe's API (one GET per booking). Add a `limit` query param or chunk + sleep between calls.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L2865-2866 stripe_webhook fix is correct and minimal: `ids = await _capture_off_session_ids(sid); update_set.update(ids)` — IDs (or empty dict) are merged INTO the same update_set that flips payment_status='paid', so they land atomically. ✅ This is the root-cause fix the task describes.

server.py L2645-2660 polling-endpoint backfill: when booking is ALREADY paid but missing stripe_payment_method_id, it re-fetches from Stripe and patches — re-reads the booking back into memory after the update so subsequent email/promo logic sees fresh data. ✅

server.py L2733-2762 _capture_off_session_ids: 10s timeout, returns {} on every error path (api_key empty, non-200, exception). No raises leak. Uses `expand[]=payment_intent` so payment_intent.payment_method is populated in one call. ✅

server.py L2783-2787 admin_backfill_saved_card: raises 400 with a customer-friendly detail ('method that can't be reused off-session') when Stripe returns no payment_method. Good UX. Note: this is the message admins will see in the frontend's 'Recover saved card' click result.
server.py L2804-2810 bulk scan filter uses `{'$in': [None, '']}` which correctly catches both missing-field and empty-string cases. ✅

server.py L2823 bulk update: `await db.bookings.update_one({'id': b['id']}, {'$set': ids})` — uses the dict directly. Safe because _capture_off_session_ids only returns keys that exist; never includes None values. ✅

Minor: bulk endpoint loops sequentially with `await` — for a clean DB this is fine, but each iteration is a 10s-timeout Stripe call. Worst case: N bookings * 10s. Consider asyncio.gather with a bounded semaphore for production.
test_credentials: Admin: support@turanelitelimo.com / TuronAdmin@2025 with 2FA bypass by overwriting admin_2fa_challenges row with bcrypt hash of '123456' (per /app/memory/test_credentials.md).
seed_data_creation: Two TEST_<uuid> paid bookings seeded mid-run with full Booking schema (full_name/phone/service_type/pickup_date/pickup_time/pickup_location/dropoff_location/passengers/vehicle_type) — one with no payment_session_id, one with bogus 'cs_test_bogus_xyz'. Both used to drive 400 paths and to populate the bulk endpoint's skipped[]. Cleanup verified: 0 TEST_ bookings remain post-run via module-scope autouse teardown fixture.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backfill saved-card feature is green end-to-end on the backend. Regression file at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration23_backfill_saved_card.py — run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL + MONGO_URL + DB_NAME loaded (the file calls load_dotenv on both /app/backend/.env and /app/frontend/.env so it self-bootstraps). Existing unpaid fixture: booking 5d6c9c49-a7b5-4bd6-97b0-86cbffff288b, driver_token 5oOuv345Fr-LxKIaj8o94g. STRIPE LIVE — the bulk endpoint will make one real GET /v1/checkout/sessions/{sid} per paid booking missing a saved card; safe (read-only) but be aware. DB currently has 0 paid bookings so the production-style recovery flow described in the task (real customer's booking → admin clicks 'Recover saved card' → real PM is retrieved) cannot be exercised here without first making a real Stripe checkout — that's an end-user/staging validation step. Frontend recovery-link UI was NOT exercised this iteration (backend-only request).
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: webhook ran first and marked booking payment_status='paid' WITHOUT capturing stripe_customer_id / stripe_payment_method_id; polling endpoint then short-circuited (`txn.status != 'paid'` already false) and also skipped capture. Result: paid booking with no saved card → auto-charge UI hidden → admin had no path to charge wait-time/damage/mid-trip-stop. Fix verified in code AND tests: (1) webhook now calls _capture_off_session_ids(sid) and merges into the same $set, (2) polling endpoint has new branch that backfills IDs even when booking is already paid, (3) admin can manually trigger backfill per-booking or bulk via two new auth-gated endpoints that share the same helper. Race condition closed at three layers.
